channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania national swimming team prepares for Africa Aquatics Championships in Algeria
item.description: Tanzania’s national swimming team is gearing up for the 2026 Africa Aquatics Swimming Championships, now set to happen in Oran, Algeria from May 5 to 10. Originally, Ghana was supposed to host the eve...

item.title: NIA to Investigate Incident of Judicial Officers Picketed by Mob in Malda, West Bengal
item.description: On April 2, 2026, the Election Commission of India formally handed over the investigation into the Malda incident, where judicial officers were surrounded by a mob, to the National Investigating Agenc...

item.title: GOP Strategists Express Confusion and Concern After Trump’s Iran Address
item.description: President Donald Trump's primetime address on the U.S. military operations in Iran has generated concern and confusion among Republican strategists and party leaders in key battleground states. While ...

item.title: Albanese Addresses Nation on Economy and US Relations Amid Cost-of-Living Concerns
item.description: In a rare national address, Prime Minister Anthony Albanese discussed measures aimed at alleviating the cost-of-living pressures affecting Australians. He outlined new initiatives including a $1 billi...

item.title: Jamie Bell and Charlie Heaton to Star in Peaky Blinders Spin-Off Set in 1950s Birmingham
item.description: A new spin-off of the acclaimed series Peaky Blinders has been announced, featuring Jamie Bell and Charlie Heaton in leading roles. Bell will take on the role of Duke 'Erasmus' Shelby, the newly appoi...

item.title: Toronto Tempo to build inaugural roster through WNBA expansion draft
item.description: The Toronto Tempo will have their first chance to assemble a roster during Friday’s WNBA expansion draft, marking the beginning of their inaugural season. Alongside fellow expansion team Portland Fire...

item.title: African Green Bank Proposal to Boost Local Renewable Technology and Industrialisation
item.description: Climate change is hitting African livelihoods hard, even though the continent has contributed very little to global emissions. Extreme weather events like floods, heatwaves, and droughts are worsening...

item.title: Tanzania Strengthens Cooperation with Development Partners to Improve National Plan Implementation
item.description: Dar es Salaam – The National Planning Commission in Tanzania met with development partners to discuss ways of strengthening collaboration and boosting the efficiency of executing national plans. The m...

item.title: National campaigns urge vigilance to safeguard children during Easter
item.description: As Easter approaches in South Africa, the Hold My Hand and Rethink Your Drink campaigns are emphasising the importance of child safety during the holiday period. These campaigns highlight that Easter,...

item.title: Roberto De Zerbi apologises for previous remarks about Mason Greenwood after joining Tottenham
item.description: Roberto De Zerbi, the newly appointed Tottenham Hotspur manager, has issued an apology for past remarks regarding Mason Greenwood. In his first interview since taking charge of the north London club, ...

item.title: Four Foreign Nationals Arrested in the Philippines for Financial Fraud and Online Gambling
item.description: The Bureau of Immigration (BI) recently apprehended four foreign nationals in separate operations in Muntinlupa City and Angeles City, Pampanga, for their alleged involvement in financial fraud and il...

item.title: Karachi witnesses overnight rain and strong winds across multiple areas
item.description: Karachi witnessed light to heavy rainfall accompanied by strong winds overnight on Friday. This followed a previous thunderstorm that tragically claimed six lives and injured three others due to elect...

item.title: Journey of Rakkaudella Chocolates: Kerala’s Single-Origin Bean-to-Bar Success
item.description: Kurian Johnson and his elder brother Ouseppachan Johnson have transformed their childhood dream into a thriving business with Rakkaudella Chocolates, Kerala’s first single-origin bean-to-bar chocolate...

item.title: Rethinking the Left-Right Divide in Australian Politics
item.description: The traditional political labels of 'left' and 'right' are increasingly being used more as social identifiers than as indicators of voting behaviour in Australia. According to a discussion led by Crik...

item.title: Governor Sule Announces 2026 Nasarawa Investment Summit to Strengthen Economic Reforms
item.description: Governor Abdullahi Sule of Nasarawa State don announce the 2026 Nasarawa Investment Summit wey go hold for May 6–7, 2026. Di summit go feature the 'Lafia Declaration', wey go make sure say reforms for...

item.title: Gulf States Urge UN to Authorise Force for Strait of Hormuz Security
item.description: On 2nd April 2026, the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) secretary general Jassem al-Budaiwi urged the UN Security Council to allow the use of force to safeguard the Strait of Hormuz from Iranian actions...

item.title: Rapper Rougé makes acting return as a vengeful character on Scandal!
item.description: Rapper Rougé, born Deko Barbara-Jessica Wedi and an alumna of the University of Pretoria, is making her acting comeback with a prominent role on the popular South African soapie Scandal!. She steps in...

item.title: Urgent need for a long-term Australian fuel security plan
item.description: Michelle Grattan examines the current fuel crisis in Australia and its implications for the Albanese government. She highlights how the government and opposition have reacted differently, with the Coa...

item.title: INEC Defends Chairman Amupitan, Affirms Independence Amid Resignation Calls
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) don confirm say dem no go gree make Chairman Professor Joash Amupitan resign, after some political actors dey accuse am of bias. According to INEC,...

item.title: Funeral director pleads guilty to preventing burials and theft of charitable funds
item.description: A funeral director in Hull, Robert Bush, has pleaded guilty to 30 counts of preventing lawful and decent burials, as well as one count of theft from 12 charities. During a police raid in March 2024, a...
